def:2.4.1.summary: Algebraic groupoid (resp. category): a groupoid (resp. category) in the category of schemes. Foundational categorical-algebraic-geometry input the rest of the paper builds on.

prop:2.11.1.summary: Effective Cartier divisor structure: for $D \in \mathrm{Div}_+(\mathscr{X})$, $\mathcal{O}_{\mathscr{X}} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{O}_D$ admits a kernel $\mathcal{O}_{\mathscr{X}}(-D)$ with prescribed properties. Cartier divisor calculus on c-stacks.

prop:3.5.1.summary: Canonical isomorphism of Picard stacks $\mathrm{Cone}(\mathbb{G}_a^\sharp \to \mathbb{G}_a) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathrm{Cone}(W \xrightarrow{p} W)$. Identifies divided-power vs Witt-vector descriptions of the same Picard structure — geometric heart of prismatization.

prop:3.8.3.summary: Self-duality: pairings induce isomorphisms $W_S^{(1)} \xrightarrow{\sim} \underline{\mathrm{Hom}}_W(W_S^{(1)}, W_S)$ and $W_S^{(F)} \xrightarrow{\sim} \underline{\mathrm{Hom}}_W(W_S^{(F)}, W_S)$. Witt-vector module self-duality powering the categorical equivalences below.

prop:3.9.1.summary: $(\mathbb{G}_a)_S \xrightarrow{\sim} \underline{\mathrm{Hom}}_W(W_S^{(F)}, W_S^{(F)})$ and vanishing $\underline{\mathrm{Hom}}_W(W_S^{(F)}, W_S^{(1)}) = 0$. Hom-vanishing structure of Witt-vector modules.

prop:3.10.1.summary: Extension classification: $\underline{\mathrm{Ex}}_W(W_S^{(1)}, W_S^{(F)}) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathrm{Cone}(W_S^{(F)} \to (\mathbb{G}_a)_S) = \mathrm{Cone}((\mathbb{G}_a^\sharp)_S \to (\mathbb{G}_a)_S)$. Computes the moduli of Witt-vector module extensions in Cartier-divisor terms.

def:3.12.1.summary: A $W_S$-module is invertible if it is fpqc-locally isomorphic to $W_S$. Foundational definition for the admissible-module category.

prop:3.14.2.summary: The c-stack $\mathrm{Adm}$ of admissible $W_S$-modules is algebraic and $\underline{\mathrm{Mor}}$-affine. Algebraicity of the admissible-module stack — license for treating it as a geometric object.

prop:3.15.7.summary: Refinement of $\mathrm{Adm}$: maps to $\mathrm{Adm}$ are faithfully flat; $\widetilde{\mathrm{Adm}} \to \mathrm{Adm}$ is a $\mathbb{G}_m^\sharp$-gerbe; $\mathrm{Adm}_+ \to \widetilde{\mathrm{Adm}}$ is a torsor. Layer structure of admissible-module moduli.

prop:4.5.8.summary: The monoid of effective Cartier divisors on $\Sigma$ is freely generated by $\Sigma \otimes \mathbb{F}_p$ and the divisors $\Delta_n$, $n \ge 0$. Generation of the divisor lattice on $\Sigma$.

prop:4.7.1.summary: Density: any closed substack $\mathscr{Y} \subset \Sigma$ through which $p: \mathrm{Spf}\,\mathbb{Z}_p \to \Sigma$ factors equals $\Sigma$. The minimality / density argument that pins down $\Sigma$.

def:5.2.1.summary: Definition of $\Sigma'(S)$: pairs $(M, \xi)$ where $M$ is admissible $W_S$-module and $\xi: M \to W_S$ is a primitive $W_S$-morphism. The 'prismatized' moduli stack — Drinfeld's geometric reformulation of perfect prisms.

thm:5.4.5.summary: Structure of $\Sigma'$: (i) $F': \Sigma' \to \Sigma$ is algebraic; (ii) $\Sigma'$ is $\underline{\mathrm{Mor}}$-affine; (iii) $\Sigma'$ is a formal c-stack. The headline structural theorem: prismatization $\Sigma'$ is a well-behaved geometric object.

prop:5.10.7.summary: Cartier-divisor structure on $\Sigma' \otimes \mathbb{F}_p$: $m\mathscr{Y}_+ + n\mathscr{Y}_-$ is left-fibered when $n \le pm$; not when $n \ge pm$. The threshold inequality structuring the divisor calculus.

prop:5.14.3.summary: Layer structure of $\Sigma'$: maps faithfully flat; $\widetilde{\Sigma}' \to \Sigma'$ is $\mathbb{G}_m^\sharp$-gerbe; $\Sigma'_+ \to \widetilde{\Sigma}'$ is a torsor under flat group scheme. Refinement structure mirroring $\mathrm{Adm}$ but on prismatization side.

prop:5.15.1.summary: Reduced structure: $\Sigma'_{\mathrm{red}} = \mathscr{Y}_+ + \mathscr{Y}_- \subset \Sigma' \otimes \mathbb{F}_p$; restriction $\Sigma'_{\mathrm{red}} \cap \Sigma_\pm = (\Sigma_\pm)_{\mathrm{red}}$. Underlying reduced subscheme description.

def:6.2.1.summary: $\Sigma'_{\mathrm{Rig}} := \Sigma' \times_{\mathrm{Adm}} \mathrm{Adm}_{\mathrm{Rig}}$. The 'rigid-analytic' fibre of the prismatization stack — bridge to rigid p-adic geometry / FF curve.

edges.note: Cartier-divisor calculus runs on the algebraic-groupoid foundations.
Hom computations use the self-duality structure.
Cone identification supplies the geometric extension model.
Invertible module definition is grounded in the extension calculus.
Algebraicity of $\mathrm{Adm}$ uses the invertibility / fpqc-local description.
Layer structure refines the algebraicity statement.
Cartier-divisor monoid generation uses the divisor calculus.
Density argument runs through the divisor lattice.
Structure theorem is for the moduli stack defined in def:5.2.1.
Algebraic + Mor-affine structure follows the same machinery as $\mathrm{Adm}$.
Density / minimality of $\Sigma$ underlies the formal c-stack property.
Divisor calculus on $\Sigma' \otimes \mathbb{F}_p$ specializes the structure theorem.
Layer structure runs on the formal c-stack.
Reduced structure follows from the gerbe / torsor description.
Rigid fibre defined via fibre product over $\mathrm{Adm}$.

main_result.description: Sections 5.2, 5.4. Definition of prismatization stack $\Sigma'$ + structure theorem.

categorical_foundations.description: Sections 2, 3.12. Algebraic groupoids / categories; invertible / admissible $W_S$-modules.

Witt_vector_calculus.description: Sections 3.5–3.10. Picard stacks, self-duality, extension classification — the algebraic engine.

Adm_structure.description: Sections 3.14–3.15. Algebraicity + layered gerbe structure of the admissible-module stack.

Sigma_geometry.description: Sections 4.5, 4.7. Cartier-divisor monoid generation; minimality through $\mathrm{Spf}\,\mathbb{Z}_p$.

Sigma_prime_structure.description: Sections 5.10–5.15, 6.2. Divisor calculus, layer structure, reduced subscheme, rigid fibre of $\Sigma'$.

Prismatic.title: Prisms and prismatic cohomology
Prismatic.role: The framework Drinfeld's prismatization geometrically reformulates.

Bhatt:NotesPrismatic.title: Lectures on prismatic cohomology
Bhatt:NotesPrismatic.role: Companion lecture notes Drinfeld's framework relates to.

Lurie:SAG.title: Spectral algebraic geometry
Lurie:SAG.role: Foundations of algebraic / formal stacks used throughout.
